cmmc.framework: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 — NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 (110 Practices)

domains.name: Access Control
domains.description: Limit information system access to authorized users, processes, and devices; enforce least privilege

domains.name: Awareness & Training
domains.description: Ensure personnel are aware of security risks; provide security literacy training; provide role-based security training

domains.name: Audit & Accountability
domains.description: Create and retain system audit logs; ensure actions can be traced to individual users; review audit logs

domains.name: Configuration Management
domains.description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations; control and monitor user-installed software; apply security configuration settings

domains.name: Identification & Authentication
domains.description: Identify and authenticate organizational users and devices; enforce MFA; manage authenticator strength

domains.name: Incident Response
domains.description: Establish incident response capability; track, document and report incidents; test incident response capability

domains.name: Maintenance
domains.description: Perform maintenance on organizational information systems; control maintenance tools; perform remote maintenance securely

domains.name: Media Protection
domains.description: Protect system media (paper and digital); limit access to media; sanitize or destroy media before disposal or reuse

domains.name: Physical Protection
domains.description: Limit physical access to authorized individuals; protect and monitor the physical facility; escort visitors

domains.name: Personnel Security
domains.description: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access; ensure information is protected during and after employment actions

domains.name: Risk Assessment
domains.description: Periodically assess risk to operations and assets; scan for vulnerabilities; remediate vulnerabilities

domains.name: Security Assessment
domains.description: Periodically assess security controls; develop and implement plans of action; monitor security controls

domains.name: System & Comms Protection
domains.description: Monitor and control communications at external boundaries; employ architectural designs that promote effective security; separate user functionality from system management functionality

domains.name: System & Information Integrity
domains.description: Identify and manage information system flaws; provide protection from malicious code; monitor information systems; perform periodic scans
